lessons.title: Open Raising From Late Position
lessons.content: Late position gives you more information, so you can enter the pot with a wider range and stronger pressure on the blinds.

lessons.title: Defending the Blinds
lessons.content: When facing a steal, defend with hands that keep playability and avoid overcalling dominated spots.

questions.title: Button open raise
questions.scenario: You are on the button in a soft cash game. The players in the blinds are passive.
questions.explanation: The button should open wider because you act last postflop and steal more uncontested pots.

choices.label: Raise a wider range
choices.explanation: This uses position to win pots before the flop and keeps pressure on the blinds.

choices.label: Limp most hands
choices.explanation: Limping gives up initiative and misses profitable steals.

choices.label: Fold everything except premiums
choices.explanation: That range is far too tight for the button.

questions.title: Small blind defense
questions.scenario: The button opens and the action folds to you in the small blind.
questions.explanation: Defend selectively. Keep hands that can realize equity well and avoid calling with weak dominated hands.

choices.label: Call with a balanced defend range
choices.explanation: A disciplined defend keeps enough playability without overdefending.

choices.label: Call every suited hand
choices.explanation: Too many marginal hands make postflop decisions harder and leak value.

choices.label: Fold all but aces and kings
choices.explanation: That is far too tight against a button steal.
